541 Central Ave E, Edgewater, MD 21037, USA

I've only seen it while in a moving vehicle, but it appears to be some kind of American persimmon. the tree was visible with fruit still hanging after all the leaves dropped in late September. Access: Private.

Quiet Waters Park; walk in for free or pay $5 to drive in. Easy access on both sides of the paved path,with the densest bushes being from the dog park on down to the loop that goes to the water. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-unknown.

It looks like the county cleared some overgrown bushes in the area and now several pawpaw trees are visible from the street that were previously hidden. might not be easily accessible, but there is a small shoulder to pull over.
Out back in the woods. Just a few trees
Mountain man neighbor I had told me there were bunches of pawpaw trees in these woods. Haven't been to verify Access: Public.
serviceberries planted in the parking lot of the central elementary school between the rows. plants infected with cedar apple rust, some fruit appear uninfected.

2 fairly large fig trees along the northwest side of 128 lubrano Drive. I pulled a slightly underripe fig off the smaller tree and it tasted good. there were many more unripe figs still growing on the trees. Access: Private. Falling Fruit season: September-Oct
Some kind of Asian persimmon. Maybe a Saijo. it is in the landscape planting of an office building behind the Italian market. Access: Private but overhanging. Falling Fruit season: October-November.
Falling Fruit access: Private but overhanging.
